arts.ttl: Arsenal news: Bukayo Saka update issued as Gunners respond to Real Madrid transfer raid
arts.sum: Arsenal are preparing for Saturday's trip to Bournemouth in their first game after the international break, with boss Mikel Arteta sweating on the fitness of a number of his stars

arts.ttl: Transfer Talk: Liverpool, Man City battling to sign Eze and Wharton
arts.sum: Crystal Palace duo Eberechi Eze and Adam Wharton are being targeted by both Liverpool and Man City. Transfer Talk has the latest.

arts.ttl: How England would line up under Thomas Tuchel
arts.sum: Crystal Palace star Marc Guehi was arguably England's most impressive asset during their Euro 2024 charge in Germany and would earn himself a spot in Tuchel's squad too. Rice's outings in the Premier League with the Gunners show enough potential to star in England's midfield, driving the ball forward while having the ability to track back and help out in defence when needed.

arts.ttl: Finland 1-3 England: Player ratings as Three Lions cruise to victory in Helsinki
arts.sum: Arsenal's Declan Rice solidified England's victory in the latter stages of the game, tapping in a ball from Ollie Watkins to fire a low ball into the back of the net - making it 3-0. GK: Dean Henderson - 6/10 - The shot-stopper was handed his first England star against Finland and worked hard to impress.

arts.ttl: Trent Alexander-Arnold ability to shine in new role unveiled by impressive England statistic
arts.sum: Alexander-Arnold, Bellingham and Jack Grealish just in front drove excellent play down the left side of the pitch, shifting almost all of England's attacking advancements onto their side. Trent Alexander-Arnold was handed a completely new position for England's clash with Finland on Sunday night, but showed his versatility by making the second most touches into the final third.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola addresses England manager speculation and Man City future
arts.sum: Manchester manager Pep Guardiola addresses his contract situation and rumours linking him with the England manager's job.

arts.ttl: ‘Anything can happen’: Guardiola discusses England job link on Italian TV
arts.sum: Manchester City manager is out of contract next year Roy Keane urges Football Association to ‘go for best’ Pep Guardiola has suggested “anything can happen” with his future as Roy Keane urged the Football Association to pursue the Manchester City manager. Guardiola, who has won six Premier League titles and the Champions League since taking over at the Etihad Stadium in 2016, is out of contract at the end of the current campaign. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Roy Keane tears into Trent Alexander-Arnold and explains why he will be 'punished' against 'better teams' - despite England star's stunning free-kick goal against Finland
arts.sum: The Liverpool star doubled England's lead with a gorgeous strike from 25 yards in the second half, helping Lee Carsley get back to winning ways.

arts.ttl: England break national team winning record with Nations League victory over Finland
arts.sum: England set a new national team winning record with their 3-1 success over Finland in League B Group 2 of the UEFA Nations League.

arts.ttl: Jack Grealish reveals he lost £500 bet with England team-mate DURING Finland match
arts.sum: England defender Trent Alexander-Arnold scored a stunning free-kick in Sunday's 3-1 win over Finland - after Jack Grealish bet him £500 that he wouldn't find the back of the net
